title: CLI verbs recovery scan|window + fail-open status recovery block + registry schemas
action: Wire recovery.mjs into the CLI (edit skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs as source AND keep .bee/bin/bee.mjs identical — bee.mjs is not mirror-guarded by test, so copy it yourself and diff to prove byte-identity). Add HANDLERS entries recovery.scan and recovery.window + a GROUP_USAGE_FALLBACKS line for the recovery group. recovery scan [--json]: run detectCrashCandidates, print candidates (session id, lane/feature, last_heartbeat, transcript path or null, last transcript activity). recovery window --session <id> [--json]: from the bare session id the handler re-derives everything itself — read the session record, resolve the transcript via perf.mjs resolveTranscript, compute sinceTs via lastDurableSettlement (lane-scoped, global fallback, started_at when none), then computeMiningWindow, then buildMiningPrompt — and emits {transcript, since_ts, event_count, window_truncated, prompt}; the orchestrator dispatches the miner worker with prompt; the CLI never calls any LLM. Register both command schemas in command-registry.mjs (both mirror copies) WITHOUT importing recovery.mjs there (schemas only — perf import discipline). Status: add buildRecoveryBlock(root) to bee.mjs modeled exactly on buildReviewBlock (bee.mjs:216-248): own try/catch, on any error return empty shape {candidates: [], degraded: true}; insert its call beside buildReviewBlock (~:421) and the recovery key into the returned status object (~:470). Empty candidates on Codex/no-store per D2. Extend templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs: recovery scan with zero sessions => empty + exit 0; scan with a crafted stale session + fixture transcript => candidate listed; window on that candidate => bounded window + prompt containing the D5 clauses; status --json carries recovery block, and recovery.degraded never breaks status. RED-first: record the failing test output (red_failure_evidence) before implementing.

truths: recovery scan and recovery window are registered in command-registry.mjs and dispatch through HANDLERS
status --json always carries a recovery block; any internal error degrades to {candidates:[], degraded:true} and never breaks status (fail-open like review)
recovery window emits the worker prompt; no LLM call happens in the CLI (D4)
templates/bee.mjs and .bee/bin/bee.mjs are byte-identical after the edit (prove with cmp/diff in the done report)
prohibitions: no auto-dispatch of mining from status or scan (D2: mining never auto-runs)
no new top-level status fields other than the recovery block

trace.outcome: Wired recovery.mjs into the CLI: recovery scan/window verbs + HANDLERS entries, command-registry.mjs schemas (no recovery.mjs import there), and a fail-open status.recovery block modeled on buildReviewBlock. templates/bee.mjs and .bee/bin/bee.mjs kept byte-identical (copy + cmp); command-registry.mjs mirrored the same way. test_bee_cli.mjs extended with 6 new checks (zero-session scan, candidate scan, bounded window + D5 prompt clauses, unknown-session refusal, status carries recovery, status fail-open on a corrupt session record) plus 'recovery' added to the 3 existing group-allowlists/bijection list.
trace.verify_output: test_bee_cli: 218 passed, 0 failed; test_recovery: 29 passed, 0 failed; test_lib_mirror: 4/4 PASS (templates/lib and .bee/bin/lib byte-identical, 24 files; hooks 10 files) -> exit 0

checks.evidence: bee.mjs HANDLERS adds recovery.scan:handleRecoveryScan,recovery.window:handleRecoveryWindow (~3931); command-registry.mjs adds recovery.scan/recovery.window schemas (@@ -1454)

checks.id: status always carries recovery block; fail-open candidates:[],degraded:true
checks.evidence: buildRecoveryBlock try/catch->{candidates:[],degraded:true} (bee.mjs:257-272); called in buildStatus + recovery key (bee.mjs:444,494); corrupt-session test exits 0 candidates:[]

checks.id: recovery window emits prompt, no LLM in CLI (D4)
checks.evidence: handleRecoveryWindow re-derives via readSession/resolveTranscript/lastDurableSettlement/computeMiningWindow/buildMiningPrompt; returns {transcript,since_ts,event_count,window_truncated,prompt}; no dispatch; test asserts prompt leads [bee-tier: generation]
cmp both bee.mjs IDENTICAL; cmp both command-registry.mjs IDENTICAL
grep recovery command-registry.mjs: only comment line 1459 must NOT import lib/recovery.mjs; no import statement

checks.id: prohibition: no auto-dispatch of mining from status or scan (D2)
checks.evidence: handleRecoveryScan+buildRecoveryBlock only call detectCrashCandidates; neither dispatches a worker nor calls buildMiningPrompt

checks.id: prohibition: no new top-level status fields besides recovery
checks.evidence: only +recovery added to buildStatus returned object (bee.mjs:494); window fields local to handleRecoveryWindow
D1 reused; D3 sinceTs lane->global->started_at; D5 prompt clauses asserted; verify re-run test_bee_cli 218/218, test_recovery 29/29, test_lib_mirror 24+10 byte-identical
